title: rubus × mollifolius
intro: Rubus × mollifolius, also known as the hybrid blackberry, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between the Himalayan blackberry (Rubus armeniacus) and the Pacific blackberry (Rubus ursinus). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus × mollifolius include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and jams, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and nutritional benefits.
